title: Effect of good faith dispute as to extinction or expiration of right
doc_html:
§208.  Effect of good faith dispute as to extinction or expiration of right

The former owner of a mineral right other than a mineral lease is not liable for damages or attorney's fees under Article 207 if there is a good faith dispute as to whether prescription has accrued, the term of the right has expired, or it has been otherwise extinguished.  

Acts 1974, No. 50, §208, eff. Jan. 1, 1975.

§208.  Effect of good faith dispute as to extinction or expiration of right
The former owner of a mineral right other than a mineral lease is not liable for damages or attorney's fees under Article 207 if there is a good faith dispute as to whether prescription has accrued, the term of the right has expired, or it has been otherwise extinguished.
Acts 1974, No. 50, §208, eff. Jan. 1, 1975.
